method_atom: An exact symbolic algorithm that finds a finite family of sample points meeting every connected component of the solution set of a system of equations whose defining functions factor through a low-dimensional intermediate map of small rank k, so that the cost grows only polynomially in the ambient number of variables n and exponentially only in the small structural parameter k; it proceeds by adjoining infinitesimals to the base coefficient domain, computing on the perturbed set, and then recovering the original components by a limit taken through an explicit isomorphism between the perturbed and unperturbed solution sets, and as a by-product bounds the number of connected components.
object_atom: The real zero set Z = Z(p(Q(X)), K^n) of a degree-d polynomial p composed with a quadratic map Q: K^n -> K^k, defined over a computable subring D of a real closed field K.
brief_reason: What transfers is the general principle of exploiting a small intermediate-map dimension plus infinitesimal-perturbation-and-limit machinery to sample and count components at cost polynomial in ambient dimension; what is domain-specific is the real algebraic set defined by a quadratic map over a real closed field, and the split is partly fused because the explicit-isomorphism limit step relies on the quadratic structure of Q.
